title: Ask questions about futures data in plain english
author: Least-Salt-3275
selftext:
So, about a month ago, I was chatting with a friend who trades futures. At some point, we started talking about AI agents and all that, but my friend was pretty skeptical because, according to him, all his attempts to feed GPT with data had failed, and the output he got was very plausible bullshit.

I thought it was a good challenge and decided to try to make an assistant that doesn't lie about numbers and understands human language... basically, I decided to try separating AI from numbers and turning it into not a core but just an interface.

The result was barb. Well, to be honest, it's not quite there yet, but I hope it will be, although that's not certain.

So, here's what I've managed to do so far, and I think it's ready to be shared:

* You can ask anything about 31 futures (with historical data from 2008).
* You can ask for a backtest.

You can't ask about several instruments in one question yet. I'm not sure if it's necessary, but it would probably be useful, I don't know.

Judging by the logs, my friend uses Barb almost every day, but one is not enough for me. To understand whether this makes any sense and is useful, share it with friends who might find it useful.

It's free, but I may limit the number of messages per day a little so as not to go broke.

[https://getbarb.trade/](https://getbarb.trade/)

Upvote1Downvote2Go to comments
